title: Photography
text:
As a wizard and scholar of Middle-earth, I have been studying the magic of the natural world for centuries. Through my self-portraits, I aim to capture the essence of my own being and reflect on my own journey through time. Each photograph is a reflection of my own experiences and emotions. Through my photography, I hope to offer a glimpse into my life as a scholar and adventurer, and inspire others to reflect on their own journeys through the world.

title: Projects
text:
arXiv Preprint | Code
Over the centuries, I have devoted countless hours to deciphering and translating the ancient scripts and dialects of the various peoples of Middle-earth. My goal is to gain a deeper understanding of the cultures and histories of these peoples by studying their languages. Currently, I am working on a monograph that explores the linguistic roots of the Elvish languages. Through extensive research and analysis, I hope to shed light on the connections between the different dialects of Elvish and their origins. This project has been particularly challenging, as Elvish is a complex and nuanced language, but I am determined to see it through to completion.

title: Projects
section: The History of the War of the Ring
text:
The History of the War of the Ring

arXiv Preprint | Code
I am creating a comprehensive and detailed history of the conflict that goes beyond the surface-level events. By gathering information from a variety of sources, including my own memories, written accounts, and oral histories, I hope to shed new light on this important period in Middle-earth’s history and provide valuable insights into the motivations and actions of the various players involved.

title: Writing Projects
text: Here is a working collection of some of my writing. I’ve only recently joined a lab and become serious about developing as a modern scientist, so I currently don’t have any scientific publications to share, but that will come with time. For now, feel free to read through some of the creative pieces included at this page.

title: Writing Projects
text:
Featured Work:

The Expansion Event
An in-progress, formally experimental fiction piece that was originally created during an independent, avant-garde fiction writing thesis project at Boston College. I’m working on converting this beginning work into a proper fiction novel, which I hope can one day be used as the foundation for an animated series.




Final Days Radio
A short story written during an Avant-Garde Fiction Writing Class that I intend to include in a collection of related short stories.

title: Hi! I’m Josh. Welcome.
text:
A Quick Introduction


Your browser does not support the video tag. 
I currently work at Case Western Reserve University as a Research Assistant in the Wilson Lab, which began operating at CWRU only this year; I’m the first lab member besides Dr. Kirk Wilson! We are currently pursuing the development of a novel clinical assay involving high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) blood-spot samples used in neonatal screening. Ideally, we will be able to patent an approach that drastically improves the stability of transported blood and urine samples, which will then save patients and providers lots of time, money, and headache.
Thanks to the university tuition benefit that is afforded to full-time employees, I also study as a part-time Biostatistics Master’s student at the university. I was a creative writer and English major as an undergraduate at Boston College but I have been interested in scientific research for many years, and I took all of the pre-medical courses required to go to medical school despite not having any intention of becoming a physician. I worked for about two years after graduation on the data management team at a contract research organization called IQVIA, where I was exposed to Biostatistics and developed a curiosity for the field that led me to Cleveland.
Rock climbing, competitive gaming, and guitar-playing are my main joys in life. I’ve consistently ranked in the top 5 Pyke players in North America for the past five years and I could talk to anyone all day about League of Legends or Project Zomboid, my favorite games of all time.
If you’d like to reach out to me, feel free to contact my email at jj.silalahi21@gmail.com, or connect with me at https://www.linkedin.com/in/josh-writes/
